spec_match.rationale: Concept exercises the relevant API surface (fetch(, document.querySelector, translate() with runtime script (~1744 chars) wired to live controls, in line with the feature summary.

interactive.rationale: Some controls present but the interactive surface is thin (4 buttons / 0 inputs / 0 selects). Hard to call it hands-on.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Concept lede matches the feature-index promise on key terms (each, element, html, input, method, side, target, unsafe, variants).

edge_cases.rationale: Page references 10 edge case / failure / fallback hits and the control surface has room to exercise distinct branches, so the happy path is not the only one shown.

references.rationale: ChromeStatus link is present but the page has no dedicated `references` section — the self-heal contract is fragile.

design_system.rationale: All colours come via 59 CSS-variable references, no raw hex, no button background override without a colour override, and not a static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Expand the interactive surface beyond one or two buttons
openQuestions.detail: Add controls that vary the parameters the spec exposes — toggles, ranges, selects — so the user can drive the demo through several distinct states instead of a single one-shot trigger.

openQuestions.title: Wrap references in a labelled `references` section
openQuestions.detail: Per CLAUDE.md rule #3, the chromestatus link is the self-heal anchor and should sit inside a clearly-labelled `references` section so the recovery script can find it reliably.

openQuestions.title: Manually verify against the spec text
openQuestions.detail: Reviewer-v1-subagent based the spec_match verdict on the chromestatus summary ("Expose multiple HTML setting methods that provide a coherent story for dynamically inserting markup into an existing document.  - Positional methods (before/after/append/prepend/replaceWith) that take HTML as argument, effectively replacing") because reading the full spec for every concept was out of budget. A human or follow-up agent should diff the demo's actual behaviour against https://github.com/WICG/declarative-partial-updates/blob/main/dynamic-markup-revamped-explainer.md#resulting-api to catch anything chromestatus glossed over.

summary: Concept ships ~1744 chars of script across 4 button(s) and 0 other control(s). hits 4 pass / 2 partial across the six rubric criteria with no outright fails.
